steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The equation $y = -4.9(x - 9.1)^2 + 10{,}700$ is in vertex form $y = a(x - h)^2 + k$. The vertex is $(h, k) = (9.1, 10{,}700)$. Because $a = -4.9 < 0$, the parabola opens downward, so the vertex is the maximum. The question asks which statement correctly interprets this.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A: states the maximum height is 9.1 meters. That confuses the two coordinates — 9.1 is the time, not the height. ✗
  • Choice B: states the plane reached its maximum height of 10,700 meters at 9.1 seconds. That matches the vertex exactly. ✓
  • Choice C: states the maximum height is 10,700 meters at $x = 0$. The vertex occurs at $x = 9.1$, not $x = 0$. ✗
  • Choice D: states the minimum height is 10,700 meters. The parabola opens downward, so the vertex is a maximum, not a minimum. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
